subject: Bahasa Inggris
curriculumNote: Draft orisinal berbasis kompetensi umum Kurikulum Merdeka; cocokkan kembali dengan ATP dan materi yang telah diajarkan di cabang.

questions.indicator: Identifying the main idea of a short text
questions.question:
Read the text "Recycling Project". Our class started a recycling project last month. Students separate paper, plastic, and metal into different bins. The collected materials are sent to a recycling center every Friday, reducing waste around the school.
What is the main idea of the text?

options.A: A class recycling project reduces school waste
options.B: A list of objects owned by the students
options.C: A problem that has no solution
options.D: An invitation to cancel an activity

questions.explanation: The entire paragraph focuses on a class recycling project reduces school waste.

questions.question: In the text "Recycling Project", what object or activity is discussed?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly discusses paper, plastic, and metal.

questions.question: The word "separate" is closest in meaning to ...

options.A: a place that cannot be entered
options.B: an action done without a reason

questions.explanation: In the paragraph, "separate" means put into different groups.

questions.question: In a summary of "Recycling Project", the phrase "this careful action" refers to ...

options.C: an unrelated event outside the text
options.D: a plan that was never mentioned

questions.explanation: The phrase points to the main object or action discussed, namely paper, plastic, and metal.

questions.indicator: Using the correct verb form for a habitual action
questions.question: Each student ... the plan carefully every week.
questions.explanation: A singular subject in a habitual action takes the simple-present form "reviews".

questions.indicator: Using a verb for an action connected to the present
questions.question: The team ... three sources before publishing the report.
questions.explanation: The singular collective subject takes "has checked" for a completed action relevant to the present.

questions.question: Announcement: "The final review session starts at 2 p.m. in Room 3. Bring your draft and source list." What must students bring?
questions.explanation: The announcement explicitly asks students to bring their draft and source list.

questions.indicator: Drawing a logical inference from a text
questions.question: Based on "Recycling Project", what attitude do the students show?
questions.explanation: The actions described show a responsible and thoughtful attitude.

questions.question: Why does the writer present the text "Recycling Project"?

options.A: To explain an activity and the reason it matters

questions.explanation: The paragraph explains the activity and its benefit or purpose.

questions.indicator: Selecting an appropriate response in an academic discussion
questions.question: A classmate says, "I found two sources with different conclusions." What is the best response?

options.A: Choose the shorter source without reading it.
options.C: Assume every conclusion is equally accurate.
options.D: Let's compare their evidence and publication context.

questions.explanation: Comparing evidence and context is a relevant critical response.
